arts.ttl: Why PSG are ready to end Champions League wait - Sutton
arts.sum: The Monday Night Club's Chris Sutton explains why he think PSG will win the Champions League this season.

arts.ttl: Luis Enrique: "Tomorrow’s match will be very intense"
arts.sum: The Paris Saint-Germain coach spoke at his pre-match press conference and with PSG TV before the second leg of the UEFA Champions League quarter-final against Aston Villa FC, on Tuesday 15 April at 21:00 (in France, 20:00 in the UK) at Villa Park. "The objectives are very clear: to solve the problems that this Aston Villa team will pose for us during the match, and to win the game.

arts.ttl: Ousmane Dembélé: "I'm really enjoying myself in this team"
arts.sum: On the eve of the UEFA Champions League quarter-final second leg against Aston Villa FC, on Tuesday 15 April at 21:00 in France (20:00 in the UK) at Villa Park, the Paris Saint-Germain forward appeared at the pre-match press conference and answered questions from PSG TV. "I believe I'm one of the key players in the team, but I've stayed the same, as always.

arts.ttl: Five who played for both: Aston Villa and PSG
arts.sum: Aston Villa and PSG face off again tonight in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final clash. The French champions take a 3-1 lead to Birmingham and are looking to put down a marker as one of the favourites to win the whole competition. But Villa will be well up for the challenge in […] The post Five who played for both: Aston Villa and PSG first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa v PSG – how to follow
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Paris Saint-Germain - Tue, Apr 15th 2025, 20:00 Aston Villa supporters can follow our UEFA Champions League quarter-final second leg against Paris Saint-Germain across the club’s official channels. Unai Emery’s side host PSG at Villa Park on Tuesday night, with the French club holding a two-goal advantage from last week's first leg. Kick-off in B6 is at 8pm. Kate Tracey, Alan McInally, Ahmed Elmohamady and Lee Hendrie will bring you ‘Matchday Live’ on VillaTV, with the pre-match action getting underway pitchside from the stadium between 6.30-7.15pm. Fans can watch all the claret and blue build-up live for free across VillaTV, X, Facebook and YouTube. Mark Regan and Ian Taylor will then bring you free live audio commentary of the Champions League encounter from 7.45pm on VillaTV and the Villa App (iOS / Google Play). Following the conclusion of the action, our post-match live show will return pitchside 15 minutes after the full-time whistle at Villa Park. We’ll analyse all the crucial action and bring you post-match reaction to what promises to be a memorable night! You can also keep up-to-date and not miss a moment by following the club’s live ‘Match Centre and Blog’ on our official website and App, as well as in-depth coverage throughout across our social media channels (X, Facebook, Instagram and TikTok). Aston Villa pre and post-match show, audio commentary, build-up, blog, live updates, team news, photos, videos, match report, interviews, reaction, highlights and more - make sure you don’t miss a pass, tackle or goal across the club’s official channels! UTV
